title: Pods replayed from generated file is broken with volumes
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->


/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
When I create a container in a pod that uses a volume and save it to a kube file (via podman generate kube <pod> -f myfile.yaml) and the wipe out the original pod and replay it via "podman play kube myfile.yaml" the container will not run (even though it did the first time).
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
MANUALLY CREATING A POD AND CONTAINER USING VOLUMES
=============================================================================================
$ podman pod create --name mysql --publish xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3306:3306
151ea4cee193e860bce2e707362270347f8167de70b7e2ee5fd7d63b9a031503
$ podman run -dt --pod mysql --name mysql-p1  -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=XXXXXXXXX \
> --volume /opt/storage/podman/data/mysql-p1/my.cnf:/etc/my.cnf \
> --volume /opt/storage/podman/data/mysql-p1/data:/var/lib/mysql \
> -d docker.io/mysql/mysql-server
858c1fb3fea63b44f41e60abad137bdfb7e0ea19aaa3beb6aafa2f5daf536879
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                COMMAND  CREATED         STATUS             PORTS                          NAMES
04cf0ddc15ca  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                          19 seconds ago  Up 10 seconds ago  xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3306->3306/tcp  151ea4cee193-infra
858c1fb3fea6  docker.io/mysql/mysql-server:latest  mysqld   11 seconds ago  Up 10 seconds ago  xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3306->3306/tcp  mysql-p1
$ podman exec -it mysql-p1 /bin/bash
bash-4.2# mysql -u root -p
Enter password: 
Welcome to the MySQL monitor.  Commands end with ; or \g.
Your MySQL connection id is 9
Server version: 8.0.21 MySQL Community Server - GPL
Copyright (c) 2000, 2020, Oracle and/or its affiliates. All rights reserved.
Oracle is a registered trademark of Oracle Corporation and/or its
affiliates. Other names may be trademarks of their respective
owners.
Type 'help;' or '\h' for help. Type '\c' to clear the current input statement.
mysql> 
STORAGE DIRECTORY CONENTS
/opt/storage/podman/data/mysql-p1/data$ ls
 auto.cnf        ca-key.pem       '#ib_16384_0.dblwr'   ib_logfile0     mysql        mysql.sock.lock      server-cert.pem   undo_002
 binlog.000001   ca.pem           '#ib_16384_1.dblwr'   ib_logfile1     mysql.err    performance_schema   server-key.pem
 binlog.000002   client-cert.pem   ib_buffer_pool       ibtmp1          mysql.ibd    private_key.pem      sys
 binlog.index    client-key.pem    ibdata1             '#innodb_temp'   mysql.sock   public_key.pem       undo_001
GENERATE A KUBE FILE
podman generate kube mysql -f ./mysql.yaml
NOW I REMOVE IT AND REMOVE THE DATA FROM THE UNDERLYING STORAGE TO START OVER
============================================================================================
Rebuild all from the kube file
$ podman play kube mysql.yaml 
Trying to pull docker.io/mysql/mysql-server:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d92ed785684c skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 331a4f2ecf4b skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e945e9180309 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob c854b862275e [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 8a3a24ad33 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Pod:
e3311b1bd4e3640392b7d099e2defe38b9283bea34deccabf3a884808b1b2b90
Container:
ea4128bd0ad0a9906a63a4400f320884e6093c9d3a95a58ef0d030b9e2de9988
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                 COMMAND  CREATED         STATUS             PORTS                          NAMES
bbc2c6e12d35  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2           12 seconds ago  Up 11 seconds ago  xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3306->3306/tcp  e3311b1bd4e3-infra
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                COMMAND  CREATED         STATUS                     PORTS                          NAMES
bbc2c6e12d35  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                          35 seconds ago  Up 34 seconds ago          xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3306->3306/tcp  e3311b1bd4e3-infra
ea4128bd0ad0  docker.io/mysql/mysql-server:latest  mysqld   34 seconds ago  Exited (1) 33 seconds ago  xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx:3306->3306/tcp  mysql-mysql-p1

```

**Describe the results you received:**
Results in the pod not running with the following reason
```
$ podman logs mysql-mysql-p1
2020-09-17T16:41:03.141921Z 0 [System] [MY-010116] [Server] /usr/sbin/mysqld (mysqld 8.0.21) starting as process 1
2020-09-17T16:41:03.150552Z 1 [ERROR] [MY-011011] [Server] Failed to find valid data directory.
2020-09-17T16:41:03.150774Z 0 [ERROR] [MY-010020] [Server] Data Dictionary initialization failed.
2020-09-17T16:41:03.150837Z 0 [ERROR] [MY-010119] [Server] Aborting
2020-09-17T16:41:03.151583Z 0 [System] [MY-010910] [Server] /usr/sbin/mysqld: Shutdown complete (mysqld 8.0.21)  MySQL Community Server - GPL.

Data Directory contents
----------------------------------------------------------
/opt/storage/podman/data/mysql-p1/data$ ls -lh
total 0
-rw-r----- 1 165562 165562 0 Sep 17 12:41 binlog.index

```

**Describe the results you expected:**
The pod and container that was described in the yaml file from the original should have manifested and been running

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
podman inspect output

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes, also reached out to the community in kubernetes #cri-o channel and was told to open a ticket.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Yaml file generated and played
```
# Generation of Kubernetes YAML is still under development!
#
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-2.0.6
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2020-09-16T23:33:12Z"
  labels:
    app: mysql
  name: mysql
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - mysqld
    env:
    - name: PATH
      value: /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
    - name: TERM
      value: xterm
    - name: container
      value: podman
    - name: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD
      value: xxxxxxxxx
    - name: HOSTNAME
      value: mysql
    image: docker.io/mysql/mysql-server:latest
    name: mysql-p1
    ports:
    - containerPort: 3306
      hostIP: xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx
      hostPort: 3306
      protocol: TCP
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
      capabilities: {}
      privileged: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
      seLinuxOptions: {}
    tty: true
    volumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /etc/my.cnf
      name: opt-storage-podman-data-mysql-p1-my.cnf
    - mountPath: /var/lib/mysql
      name: opt-storage-podman-data-mysql-p1-data
    workingDir: /
  volumes:
  - hostPath:
      path: /opt/storage/podman/data/mysql-p1/data
      type: Directory
    name: opt-storage-podman-data-mysql-p1-data
  - hostPath:
      path: /opt/storage/podman/data/mysql-p1/my.cnf
      type: File
    name: opt-storage-podman-data-mysql-p1-my.cnf
status: {}
---
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
spec: {}
status:
  loadBalancer: {}

```

comments.body: Interested in opening a PR to fix this?
@havok4u Can you provide the `podman inspect` of a working container from `podman run` as well? We can then diff the output of both and see what `play kube` did differently

@mheon Here is the inspect data of a working mysql
```
$ podman pod list
POD ID        NAME    STATUS   CREATED         # OF CONTAINERS  INFRA ID
3876e5af82ee  mysql   Running  36 seconds ago  2                91538bd3f505

91538bd3f505  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2           41 seconds ago  Up 19 seconds ago  192.168.50.214:3306->3306/tcp  3876e5af82ee-infra
```
